content.text: # Adding an Image to an HTML Page To add an image to a webpage, use the **``** tag. ## Basic Syntax ```html ``` ## Key Attributes | Attribute | Description | |-----------|-------------| | `src` | **Required** – Specifies the path/URL of the image | | `alt` | **Required** – Alternative text (shown if image fails to load; aids accessibility) | | `width` | Sets the image width (in pixels or %) | | `height` | Sets the image height (in pixels or %) | ## Examples **1. Image from the same folder:** ```html ``` **2. Image with width and height:** ```html ``` **3. Image from a folder (relative path):** ```html ``` **4. Image from an external URL:** ```html ``` ## Notes - The `` tag is **empty** (self-closing) — it has no closing tag. - Always include the `alt` attribute for accessibility and SEO. Let me know if you'd like to learn how to make the image a clickable link! 🖼️
